data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats self-consistency if and only if (net majorities correctly saved from false rejection) > (net majorities incorrectly broken by false flags). Formally: R*(1 - P_NF) * P_flip_correct > (1 - R)*P_NF*P_break_correct, where R=critic recall on wrong candidates, P=critic precision, P_NF=prob critic flags the pool, and P_flip_correct is the probability the remaining pool, after removing true-positive flags, now yields the right answer.", "mechanism": "Two competing causal channels operate on the SAME k-candidate pool: (1) the rescue channel—critic flags genuinely wrong candidates, removing them so a previously-fragmented majority coalesces on the correct answer; (2) the erosion channel—critic false-flags correct candidates, breaking a correct majority and letting a wrong answer win. The harness strictly beats SC when the rescue flux exceeds the erosion flux. The observed v4-pro hard-set (recall=1.0, precision=0.125, net=0) is the equilibrium where these fluxes exactly cancel; the v4-flash easy-set (all arms 0.958) is the trivial regime where base error is too low for rescue to matter (P_NF ≈ 0). The decisive variable is critic precision as a function of pool difficulty: precision collapses on hard items because the critic itself struggles, producing the false-flag erosion that kills the harness.", "scope": {"covers": ["odd k in [5, 15]", "base candidate error e in (1/(k+1), 1/2)", "critic precision and recall both strictly in (0, 1)", "binary-answer QA tasks"]}, "excludes": ["e <= 1/(k+1) (majority always correct, no rescue possible)", "e >= 1/2 (majority always wrong, harness cannot manufacture correctness from a wrong pool)", "perfect-precision critic (trivially dominates)", "continuous-generation tasks without discrete majority semantics"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Experiment yields a mid-zone arm (1/(k+1) < e < 1/2) where harness recall >= 0.5 AND precision >= 0.6 AND harness accuracy <= SC accuracy across ALL tested k in {7, 9, 11}", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Across all tested difficulty bins, measured (rescue flux - erosion flux) has the wrong sign relative to (harness accuracy - SC accuracy), i.e. flux model predicts harness win but SC wins or ties in 4+ of 6 bins", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "The flux-balance equation subsumes the neighbourhood's fragility-window claim (7811dbda, 2d711b2e): the window 1/(k+1) < e < 1/2 is the necessary condition for P_flip_correct > 0, but it is not sufficient—precision must also be high enough that the erosion channel does not cancel rescue. The refuted candidate (133fcf5d) likely claimed a fixed-precision/recall sufficiency without the flux inequality; the present claim adds the causal flux mechanism and the precision-vs-difficulty collapse explanation for why the observed hard-set netted to zero."}
